name: Maragogype

migrationPath.notes: Natural Typica mutation producing exceptionally large beans

description: A natural mutation of Typica discovered in Bahia, Brazil in 1870, famous for producing the largest coffee beans of any Arabica variety — sometimes called 'Elephant Bean.' Despite striking visual appeal, it is low-yielding and the cup quality is generally mild. Its size has made it a parent for crosses targeting large bean size, most notably Pacamara (Maragogype x Pacas).
